findings.evidence: `rg` confirms existing analogues: `code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/JFunction.lean:477 theorem J_transpose`, `:506 theorem JO_transpose`, and `:511 theorem JX_transpose`; Rotation.lean only adds `GridPoint.J_image_rev` and grading-level consequences.
findings.fix: Add `GridState.J_rotate (x y) : GridState.J x.rotate y.rotate = GridState.J x y` and `GridDiagram.JO_rotate`/`JX_rotate` proved from `GridState.rotate_pointSet`, `rotate_OSet`/`rotate_XSet`, and `GridPoint.J_image_rev`.
findings.issue: Rotation exposes `GridPoint.J_image_rev` but omits the corresponding `GridState.J_rotate`, `GridDiagram.JO_rotate`, and `GridDiagram.JX_rotate` lemmas, leaving users to unfold point sets while transpose has these state/diagram-level APIs.

summary: The point-set rotation lemmas are at the right level, but the PR stops short of the natural state/diagram-level J API already provided for transpose.
